findings.name: Improper Handling of Length Parameter Inconsistency
findings.description: The product parses a formatted message or structure, but it does not handle or incorrectly handles a length field that is inconsistent with the actual length of the associated data.
findings.evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java:145 - Photo constructor receives file.getSize() as fileSize but actual photoData byte array stored separately; no validation that byte array length matches declared fileSize parameter.

findings.name: Use of Hard-coded Password
findings.description: The product contains a hard-coded password, which it uses for its own inbound authentication or for outbound communication to external components.

findings.name: Missing Initialization of a Variable
findings.description: The product does not initialize critical variables, which causes the execution environment to use unexpected values.
findings.evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java:113-115 - Variables 'width', 'height', and 'photoData' initialized to null; conditionally assigned within try block. If exception occurs before assignment, variables remain null and are used downstream without null checks.

findings.name: Use of Uninitialized Variable
findings.description: The code uses a variable that has not been initialized, leading to unpredictable or unintended results.
findings.evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java:83 - file.getContentType() can return null per MultipartFile API; calling .toLowerCase() on potential null value will throw NullPointerException. Also lines 125-126: width/height passed to setWidth/setHeight without null validation when image is null.

findings.name: Unchecked Input for Loop Condition
findings.description: The product does not properly check inputs that are used for loop conditions, potentially leading to a denial of service or other consequences because of excessive looping.
findings.evidence: HomeController.java:57-68 - Loop iterates over 'files' List<MultipartFile> from @RequestParam without bounds checking maximum file count; no limit on number of files an attacker can upload in a single request.

findings.name: Memory Allocation with Excessive Size Value
findings.description: The product allocates memory based on an untrusted, large size value, but it does not ensure that the size is within expected limits, allowing arbitrary amounts of memory to be allocated.
findings.evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java:119 - file.getBytes() reads entire file into memory without per-request memory limit enforcement; multiple concurrent large file uploads could exhaust JVM heap memory (DoS risk).

findings.name: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
findings.description: The product contains hard-coded credentials, such as a password or cryptographic key.
findings.evidence: application.properties:11-12 - spring.datasource.username=photoalbum, spring.datasource.password=photoalbum; application-docker.properties:3-4 - same hardcoded credentials

findings.name: Data Access Operations Outside of Expected Data Manager Component
findings.description: The product uses a dedicated, central data manager component as required by design, but it contains code that performs data-access operations that do not use this data manager.
findings.evidence: PhotoFileController.java:38-62 - Controller directly accesses photo binary data (photoData, originalFileName, mimeType) from Photo entity instead of delegating to a dedicated service method; data access logic should be abstracted in the service layer.
